title: `libprotobuf-c0-dev` is not found
body:
[//]: # **kind bug**

There is no [`libprotobuf-c0-dev`](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/install.md#build-and-run-dependencies) on Ubuntu 18.04.

```
# apt -y search libprotobuf  
Sorting... Done
Full Text Search... Done
libprotobuf-c-dev/bionic 1.2.1-2 amd64
  Protocol Buffers C static library and headers (protobuf-c)

libprotobuf-c1/bionic 1.2.1-2 amd64
  Protocol Buffers C shared library (protobuf-c)

libprotobuf-c1-dbg/bionic 1.2.1-2 amd64
  Protocol Buffers C shared library debug symbols (protobuf-c)

libprotobuf-dev/bionic 3.0.0-9.1ubuntu1 amd64
  protocol buffers C++ library (development files)

libprotobuf-java/bionic 3.0.0-9.1ubuntu1 all
  Java bindings for protocol buffers

libprotobuf-java-format-java/bionic 1.3-1 all
  Library to serialize protobuf messages to XML, JSON and HTML

libprotobuf-lite10/bionic 3.0.0-9.1ubuntu1 amd64
  protocol buffers C++ library (lite version)

libprotobuf10/bionic 3.0.0-9.1ubuntu1 amd64
  protocol buffers C++ library
```

comments.body:
installing libprotobuf-c0-dev works on Ubuntu 18.04:

```
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
Note, selecting 'libprotobuf-c-dev' instead of 'libprotobuf-c0-dev'
The following additional packages will be installed:
  libprotobuf-c1
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  libprotobuf-c-dev libprotobuf-c1
0 upgraded, 2 newly installed, 0 to remove and 4 not upgraded.
Need to get 44.2 kB of archives.
After this operation, 157 kB of additional disk space will be used.
Do you want to continue? [Y/n] 
```

on the other hand we cannot use `libprotobuf-c-dev` as it won't work on Ubuntu 14.04
@giuseppe, what do you propose doing? Shall we remove `libprotobuf-c-dev` from the package list for Ubuntu?
@vrothberg I'd leave it as it is.  From what I can see, it works on both versions of Ubuntu

Okay. I am closing the issue as installing the package will work, see @giuseppe's output:
```
[...]
Note, selecting 'libprotobuf-c-dev' instead of 'libprotobuf-c0-dev'
[...]
```
After running `apt update` it becomes available. Sorry for the noise.
